io¶

IO¶

Catalog I/O, wavelength grid construction, and FITS output writer.

Key functions:

  • read_catalog() — loads npz/fits/csv catalogs

  • build_wavelength_grid() — constructs the target resampling grid

  • save_to_file() — writes the stacked spectra to a FITS file

spectraPyle.io.IO.build_wavelength_grid(wavelMin, wavelMax, config, z_stacking)[source]¶

Build wavelength grid (centers and bin edges) for stacking.

Parameters:

  • wavelMin (float) – Wavelength range (Å)

  • wavelMax (float) – Wavelength range (Å)

  • config (dict) – Configuration dictionary containing: - pixel_resampling_type : ‘lambda’, ‘lambda_shifted’, or ‘log_lambda’ - pixel_resampling : Δλ (Å) or Δlog10(λ)

  • z_stacking (float) – Reference redshift for stacking

Returns:

  • wavelength_centers (ndarray)

  • wavelength_bins (ndarray)

  • pixel_size (float) – Δλ (Å) or Δlog10(λ), depending on sampling

spectraPyle.io.IO.get_effective_cpu_limit()[source]¶

Return the effective CPU count considering cgroup and affinity limits.

Accounts for cgroup v1 (cpu.cfs_quota_us/period), cgroup v2 (cpu.max), and CPU affinity settings. Useful in containerized/scheduled environments.

Returns:

Number of effectively available CPUs (at least 1).

Return type:

int

spectraPyle.io.IO.output_units(config)[source]¶

Determine output physical units from configuration.

Parameters:

config (dict) – Configuration dictionary.

Returns:

  • out_unitstr

    Output unit string

  • out_unit_scale_factorfloat

    Scaling factor for units

Return type:

tuple

spectraPyle.io.IO.read_catalog(dirIn, fname, extension, mandatory_keys)[source]¶

Load a catalog file and extract mandatory columns.

Parameters:

  • dirIn (str) – Directory containing the catalog file.

  • fname (str) – Filename without extension.

  • extension ({"npz", "fits", "csv"}) – File format.

  • mandatory_keys (list of str) – Column names that must be present.

Returns:

Keys are column names, values are arrays.

Return type:

dict

Raises:

  • FileNotFoundError – If the file does not exist at dirIn/fname.extension.

  • ValueError – If a mandatory key is missing or the format is unsupported.

spectraPyle.io.IO.save_to_file(config, data_dict)[source]¶

Save stacked spectra and metadata to a FITS file.

Parameters:

  • config (dict) – Configuration dictionary containing output paths, normalization type, etc.

  • data_dict (dict) – Dictionary containing stacking results: wavelengths, fluxes, dispersions, pixel counts, etc.

Returns:

Path to the generated FITS file.

Return type:

str

spectraPyle.io.IO.z_sort(z_column_name, data_input)[source]¶

Sort the input catalog by redshift.

Parameters:

  • z_column_name (str) – Name of the column containing redshifts.

  • data_input (dict) – Input catalog (keys=column names, values=arrays).

Returns:

Input catalog sorted by increasing redshift.

Return type:

dict

spectraPyle.io.IO.z_stack(redshift, z_type, conservation)[source]¶

Determine the redshift of the stacked spectrum.

Parameters:

  • redshift (ndarray) – Redshifts of the spectra to be stacked.

  • z_type (str, int, or float) – Keyword for stacking redshift. One of: - ‘median_z’: median of redshift array - ‘minimum_z’: minimum of redshift array - ‘maximum_z’: maximum of redshift array - ‘rest_frame’: z=0 - float/int: user-defined redshift value

  • conservation (str) – Spectral quantity to conserve: ‘luminosity’ or ‘flux’.

Returns:

  • z_minfloat

    Minimum redshift of the array

  • z_maxfloat

    Maximum redshift of the array

  • z_stackingfloat

    Redshift of the stacking frame

Return type:

tuple

Filename Builder¶

Auto-generation of output FITS filenames from configuration.

Provides build_filename() which constructs descriptive, self-documenting filenames encoding instrument, grisms, redshift type, normalization, and sample size.

spectraPyle.io.filename_builder.build_filename(cfg)[source]¶

Auto-generate the output FITS filename stem from config fields.

Constructs a descriptive name encoding instrument, grisms, redshift type, normalization, and sample size so output files are self-documenting.

Parameters:

config (dict) – Flat stacking config dict.

Returns:

Filename stem (without .fits extension).

Return type:

str
